meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/sharepoint + PowerShell module refs) — admin-center paths, setting labels, and cmdlets grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: Almost all of these are tenant-wide settings in the SharePoint admin center (admin.microsoft.com/sharepoint). Most map to a single Set-SPOTenant property; a couple use their own cmdlet (idle sign-out) and are noted inline.

In the sharing UI, the label 'New and existing guests' corresponds to the PowerShell value ExternalUserSharingOnly, and 'Anyone' = ExternalUserAndGuestSharing. OneDrive's sharing level can be equal to or more restrictive than SharePoint's, never more permissive.

Several rows are verify-only — they document the current/baseline state (e.g. unmanaged sync not restricted, no sync file exclusions, Mac sync enabled) rather than a change to push. Confirm the value and record drift.
You need the SharePoint Administrator role (or Global Admin). PowerShell examples use the SharePoint Online Management Shell (Set-SPOTenant / Set-SPOBrowserIdleSignOut).

requiredSettings.label: External sharing — SharePoint
requiredSettings.value: New and existing guests (PowerShell: SharingCapability = ExternalUserSharingOnly) — authenticated external users only; anonymous 'Anyone' links disabled

requiredSettings.label: External sharing — OneDrive
requiredSettings.value: New and existing guests (equal to or more restrictive than SharePoint)

steps: SharePoint admin center → Policies → Sharing.
Set the SharePoint external sharing slider to 'New and existing guests', and the OneDrive slider to 'New and existing guests' (or tighter).
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOTenant -SharingCapability ExternalUserSharingOnly.

Note (HIGH impact, paired with the reshare policy below): this removes the 'Anyone with the link' option from the share dialog. Existing anonymous links keep working until they expire; no new ones can be created. If the client relies on anonymous links (proposals, media to clients), communicate before rollout.

requiredSettings.label: Allow guests to share items they don't own

steps: SharePoint admin center → Policies → Sharing → expand 'More external sharing settings'.
Uncheck 'Allow guests to share items they don't own'.
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOTenant -PreventExternalUsersFromResharing $true.
Note (HIGH impact): external guests who received a shared link can no longer reshare it onward. Combined with the sharing-level policy, external sharing is limited to named, authenticated guests.

requiredSettings.label: Requirement
requiredSettings.value: Relies on Conditional Access — needs Microsoft Entra ID P1/P2 (included with Business Premium)

steps: SharePoint admin center → Policies → Access control → Idle session sign-out.
Turn on 'Sign out inactive users automatically'; set sign-out = 60 minutes and warning = 5 minutes.
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOBrowserIdleSignOut -Enabled:$true -WarnAfter (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 55) -SignOutAfter (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 60).

Note (MEDIUM impact): applies org-wide to SharePoint/OneDrive in the browser only (not desktop apps) and can't be scoped to specific users — use Conditional Access sign-in frequency if you need targeting. Browser users who step away >1h are signed out and may lose unsaved in-browser edits.

requiredSettings.label: Days to retain a deleted user's OneDrive

steps: SharePoint admin center → Settings → OneDrive - Retention (the 'Retention' setting).
Set 'Days to retain files in a deleted user's OneDrive' = 60.
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOTenant -OrphanedPersonalSitesRetentionPeriod 60.
Note: invisible to active users; matters at offboarding — the clock starts when the account is deleted from Entra ID. (Unlicensed OneDrive is separately auto-archived at 93 days.)
SharePoint admin center → Settings → OneDrive - Storage limit.
Set the default storage to 1024 GB (1 TB) for all licensed users.
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOTenant -OneDriveStorageQuota 1048576 (to re-apply to an existing user: Set-SPOSite -Identity <OneDrive URL> -StorageQuotaReset).
Note: standard allocation — no user impact unless a user was previously on a lower limit. A value above a user's license cap is rounded down to that cap.

requiredSettings.label: Site creation
requiredSettings.value: Users can still create sites (site creation enabled)

steps: SharePoint admin center → Settings → Site creation.
Confirm site creation is enabled and review the default site storage limit / managed path.
This is a verify-only baseline item — no restrictive change; most users won't notice. (Tenant setting IsSiteCreationEnabled / SiteCreationDefaultStorageLimitInMb.)

requiredSettings.label: Restrict OneDrive sync on unmanaged (non-domain-joined) devices
requiredSettings.value: NOT restricted in this baseline — sync is allowed from any device (IsUnmanagedSyncAppForTenantRestricted = false)

steps: SharePoint admin center → Settings → OneDrive - Sync (the 'Allow syncing only on computers joined to specific domains' option).
Verify the domain restriction is OFF — this baseline intentionally does not restrict sync by device.
Verify-only: confirm the current state and record it. If the client later wants to restrict, this is where you'd enable the allowed-domains list.

requiredSettings.label: Block upload of specific file types (OneDrive sync)
requiredSettings.value: None excluded in this baseline (ExcludedFileExtensionsForSyncClient is empty)

steps: SharePoint admin center → Settings → OneDrive - Sync → 'Block upload of specific file types'.
Verify no file extensions are listed — all file types sync normally.
Verify-only: confirm and record. To exclude types later: Set-SPOTenant -ExcludedFileExtensionsForSyncClient 'ext1','ext2'.

requiredSettings.label: OneDrive sync client for Mac
requiredSettings.value: Enabled (IsMacSyncAppEnabled = true) — Mac users can sync normally

steps: SharePoint admin center → Settings → OneDrive - Sync.
Confirm the OneDrive sync app is allowed for Mac devices.
Verify-only: no restrictive impact; this documents that Mac sync is enabled in the baseline.
